subject: LU-2182 llapi: implementation of new llapi_layout API

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Sorry, haven't looked at the patch yet, but one important comment is that this code should go into a separate new file (e.g. liblustreapi_layout.c) that is under the LGPL as was done for liblustreapi_hsm.c.  Having a separate file helps with code organization, and also will help the transition of the whole liblustreapi library to LGPL in the future, so that it is available to all applications.

Patch Set 2:

Thank you for this new API, it really clarifies the use of lustre layout from user space. I am wondering if you could add a llapi_layout_lookup_byfid() call so the 3 ways to access a Lustre file will be covered  (path, fd, fid)

Patch Set 2: (5 inline comments)

The patch must also contains the tests for the API. It can be lfs modifications or a new specific test pgm (2nd will be better because it can also test failure and bad use cases of the API)

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

jacques-Charles, thanks for the review.  That's a good idea to add a function to lookup byfid.  I'll address your comments in the next patch set.

Not to belabor the point, but what is the utility of exposing the server's on-disk format through f[gs]etxattr? I haven't been able to find any documentation on this subject. Perhaps a debugging or recovery aid? Otherwise, why not just have both interfaces work with the user space format?

I still need to properly integrate the test suite, but in the meantime I wanted to make my other changes available for comment.

Patch Set 3 changes include:

- Expose layout generation number through the API
- Add llapi_layout_lookup_byfid
- Revised man page
- Move XATTR definitions from lustre_idl.h to lustre_user.h
- Add test program to lustre/tests directory

Patch Set 18:

(2 comments)

James, it looks like you're planning to refresh this patch.  In that case we should work together.  Though I've been remiss in finishing this up, I have been working with Andy Nelson from LANL on further refinements that I want to include.  In particular, we want to add an interface like the following, which would allow the caller to discover the actual layout a newly created file would get.

  /*
   * Like llapi_layout_by_path(), but substitute expected inherited
   * attribute values for unspecified attributes.  Normally unspecified
   * attributes have the value LL_LAYOUT_USE_FS_DEFAULT.
   */
  llapi_layout_t *llapi_layout_expected(const char *path);

Patch Set 19:

Oops. I just pushed a new patch. Yes I like to work together as well. The reason for the involvement is ORNL is very interested in the work going on for LU-4665. This is the base patch that is needed. If you compare struct llapi_layout to struct llapi_stripe_param from LU-4665 you see they are almost the same.

Patch Set 19:

(1 comment)

Go for it. I look forward to the improvements.

Patch Set 19: Code-Review-1

(15 comments)

I am concerned that this API is too closely tied to v1 and v3 layouts and will not age gracefully as new layouts (composite layouts for replication, specific striping, ...) are introduced.

I am also concerned that we are placing policy in llapi that should really be up to the MDT. Since the llapi version may not be equal to the MDT version we'll just introduce artificial limits.

Rather than adding flag that means stripe over all OSTs, the flag should mean stripe as widely as possible.

I need a bit more time to think about this.

Patch Set 19: -Code-Review

> I am concerned that this API is too closely tied to v1 and v3 layouts and will not age gracefully as new layouts (composite layouts for replication, specific striping, ...) are introduced.

Is there any documentation out there on composite layouts?  I've only heard rumors about this feature. I'd love to get some concrete details so we can make suitable accommodations in the API.

Regarding specific striping, we tried to anticipate that with llapi_layout_ost_index_set().  Currently it only supports setting stripe 0, but that limit would be removed pending server support.

> I am also concerned that we are placing policy in llapi that should really be up to the MDT. Since the llapi version may not be equal to the MDT version we'll just introduce artificial limits.

True but I don't think this concern needs to hold up an initial implementation.  As interfaces are added to export policy from the MDS the library can use them without changing the public interface.

> Rather than adding flag that means stripe over all OSTs, the flag should mean stripe as widely as possible.

Yes, I'm thinking of renaming LLAPI_LAYOUT_USE_ALL_OSTS to LLAPI_LAYOUT_WIDE.  Documentation will be updated accordingly.

parents.subject: LU-5195: hsm: delete HSM records not found on disk

commit.subject: LU-2182 llapi: implementation of new llapi_layout API
commit.message:
LU-2182 llapi: implementation of new llapi_layout API

Add a new set of llapi routines for interacting with file layouts that
hide the details of the wire-protocol data structures from the user.
Define an opaque struct llapi_layout to abstract the layout of a
lustre file and generic accessor functions to read and write it.  The
following documented functions are added the liblustreapi public
interface with accompanying man pages:

  llapi_layout_alloc - allocate a new layout
  llapi_layout_free - free memory allocated for a layout
  llapi_layout_file_create - create new file with given layout
  llapi_layout_file_open - open or create a file with given layout
  llapi_layout_get_by_path - get file layout given a path
  llapi_layout_get_by_fd - get file layout given a file descriptor
  llapi_layout_get_by_fid - get file layout given a Lustre FID
  llapi_layout_ost_index_get - get OST index associated with a stripe
  llapi_layout_ost_index_set - set OST index associated with a stripe
  llapi_layout_pattern_get - get RAID pattern of a layout
  llapi_layout_pattern_set - set RAID pattern of a layout
  llapi_layout_pool_name_get - get pool name of a layout
  llapi_layout_pool_name_set - set pool name of a layout
  llapi_layout_stripe_count_get - get stripe count of a layout
  llapi_layout_stripe_count_set - set stripe count of a layout
  llapi_layout_stripe_size_get - get stripe size of a layout
  llapi_layout_stripe_size_set - set stripe size of a layout

The layouts are read and written using fgetxattr() and fsetxattr()
instead of ioctl() to make it easier for architectures like Blue Gene
to function-ship the system calls.

Signed-off-by: Ned Bass <bass6@llnl.gov>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I35fb51055b6438ef3090f43c28a4083a66eaa907
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5302
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
